name: Windows Bootstrap Fix
goal: Fix bootstrap deadlock on Windows: cross-platform ensureSqlite3() in MCP wrappers + self-bootstrapping schema in session-init.sh

tasks.name: Cross-platform ensureSqlite3() in state-manager wrapper
tasks.description: Replace command -v sqlite3 with platform-conditional check (where.exe on Windows) and add Windows install hint

steps.description: Replace ensureSqlite3() function (lines 28-41) with cross-platform version using process.platform check
steps.expected: Function uses where.exe on win32, command -v elsewhere, includes Windows install hint
Lines 28-42 show new ensureSqlite3() with process.platform === win32 check

tasks.name: Cross-platform ensureSqlite3() in episodic-memory wrapper
tasks.description: Same cross-platform ensureSqlite3() fix as task 1, applied to episodic-memory wrapper

steps.description: Replace ensureSqlite3() function (lines 28-41) with cross-platform version using process.platform check
steps.expected: Function uses where.exe on win32, command -v elsewhere, includes Windows install hint
Lines 28-42 show new ensureSqlite3() with process.platform === win32 check

tasks.name: Self-bootstrapping schema in session-init.sh
tasks.description: Add full schema bootstrap (all 8 tables + indexes + WAL mode) before the session INSERT so hooks work even when MCP servers havent started. Also add cross-reference comment to db.ts.

steps.description: Insert schema bootstrap block between PROJECT_HASH line and session INSERT comment in session-init.sh
steps.expected: Bootstrap block with PRAGMA journal_mode=wal + all 8 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + indexes appears before session INSERT

steps.description: Add cross-reference comment to db.ts before the db.exec call at line 199
steps.expected: Comment referencing session-init.sh appears before db.exec
Schema bootstrap block visible with all 8 CREATE TABLE statements and indexes
